raw_consumer_statement:
Re : My Disputed Case ID XXXX I am a retired XXXX XXXXr ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) who is frustrated by being defrauded {$600.00} by a scarcely stocked convenience storeand by not being protected by my card issuer, Citibank. Here 's what happened... 
On my trip from XXXX to XXXX on XX/XX/year>, I went inside a convenience store, XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX in XXXX, GA, to pre-pay {$40.00} with my Citicard to fill my XXXX with gasoline. After processing my Citicard, the merchant informed me that he was unable to give me a receipt because his register was out of receipt paper. Thinking nothing of it, I went out & pumped {$40.00} of gas into my car and drove off. 
Several weeks later I received my Citicard statement with a charge of {$640.00}. When I immediately protested the overcharge to Citicard, they advised me to contact the merchant. Phoning the store XXXX, he angrily refused to give me his name ( he sounded XXXX ) and he informed me that he had no transaction receipt to give me describing the charge. On another attempted phone connection, his clerk said, XXXX is worth {$640.00} in this store. 
Also, XXXX decision to deny my {$600.00} overcharge was based on a major error. Citicard reported back incorrectly that the reason for the dispute was XXXX XXXX. This is absolutely false because no goods were involved, except {$40.00} in pumped gasoline. There was nothing else to return. I bought {$40.00} in gas and was improperly billed {$640.00}. This is absolutely absurd and an obvious case of fraudulent billing by taking advantage of an old man who likely would not be checking his bill carefully.. 
Because I had no written receipt or any paperwork to support my case, after months of dickering with multiple Citicard departments, my disputed overcharge of {$600.00} has been denied on XXXX different efforts. I am shocked that Citicard has refused to protect me as a cardholder. I am left to protest this decision. My XXXX ID is XXXX. 
Sincerely,
